Housing Units per Job.definition: The ratio of total housing units to the total number of jobs within the county.
Housing Units per Job.meaning: A value of 1.0 means there is exactly one housing unit for every job. Lower values (e.g., < 0.7) suggest a potential housing shortage relative to the workforce, while higher values suggest a 'bedroom community' where housing significantly exceeds local employment.
Housing Units per Job.source: U.S. Census Bureau ACS (Housing Units) & QWI (Employment)

People per Housing Unit.definition: The total population divided by the number of housing units.
People per Housing Unit.meaning: Represents the average number of people residing in each housing unit. Higher values may indicate larger average household sizes or potential housing overcrowding.
People per Housing Unit.source: U.S. Census Bureau ACS (American Community Survey)

Jobs per Capita.definition: Total jobs in the county divided by the total population.
Jobs per Capita.meaning: Measures employment density relative to the entire population. A value of 0.5 means there is one job for every two people in the county.

Jobs per Working Age Adult.definition: Total jobs in the county divided by the population aged 18-64.
Jobs per Working Age Adult.meaning: Measures job availability relative to the likely workforce. A value of 1.0 means there is one local job for every working-age resident.
Jobs per Working Age Adult.source: U.S. Census Bureau QWI (Jobs) & ACS (Working Age Pop)

Absolute (Net Flow).definition: The difference between the number of people commuting into the county for work and the number of residents commuting out.
Absolute (Net Flow).meaning: A positive value indicates the county is a 'net importer' of labor (a job center). A negative value indicates a 'bedroom community' that exports labor to neighboring areas.
Absolute (Net Flow).source: U.S. Census Bureau LODES (LEHD Origin-Destination Employment Statistics)

Commuter Ratio (In/Out).definition: The number of in-commuters divided by the number of out-commuters.
Commuter Ratio (In/Out).meaning: A ratio of 1.0 means the number of people coming into the county to work exactly equals the number of residents leaving to work elsewhere. Values > 1 indicate a job center; < 1 indicate a residential hub.

In-Commuter Job Share (% of Jobs).definition: The percentage of jobs in the county that are filled by people who live outside the county.
In-Commuter Job Share (% of Jobs).meaning: Measures the degree to which a county's economy depends on labor from other counties. For example, 50% means half of all local jobs are held by non-residents.

Resident Retention Share (% of Residents.definition: The percentage of working residents who also work within their home county.
Resident Retention Share (% of Residents.meaning: Measures how many residents 'stay' in the county for work. 100% means all working residents work locally; lower values indicate higher levels of out-commuting.

Average Age.definition: The weighted average age of the county population.
Average Age.meaning: An estimate of the overall age of the community based on census age-band distributions.

% Residents Under 18.definition: The percentage of the population aged 0-17.
% Residents Under 18.meaning: Indicates the proportion of children and minors in the county.

% Residents 18-22.definition: The percentage of the population aged 18-22.
% Residents 18-22.meaning: Often associated with college-aged populations or early-career starters.

% Residents 23-34.definition: The percentage of the population aged 23-34.
% Residents 23-34.meaning: Represents young professionals and the early-to-mid career workforce.

% Residents 35-49.definition: The percentage of the population aged 35-49.

% Residents 50-64.definition: The percentage of the population aged 50-64.
% Residents 50-64.meaning: Represents the late-career workforce and pre-retirement age population.

% Residents Over 65.definition: The percentage of the population aged 65 and older.
% Residents Over 65.meaning: Indicates the proportion of the retirement-age population.

% Working Age (18-64).definition: The percentage of the population aged 18-64.
% Working Age (18-64).meaning: Represents the core potential labor force of the county.
